title: Ongoing Divergence in Chinese Steel Production and Iron Ore Imports
section_path: Ongoing Divergence in Chinese Steel Production and Iron Ore Imports
section_path_text: Ongoing Divergence in Chinese Steel Production and Iron Ore Imports
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
One issue very much worthy of conversation but still often ignored is that China's iron ore imports and steel production have been diverging recently. China's steel production peaked back in May at 99.5 million tons, and then fell each month in June, July, August, and September (with September seeing production reach a low of 73.8 million tons). Image reference: Chart 1 (3).jpg | assets/2021-10-20_ongoing-divergence-in-chinese-steel-production-and-iron-ore-imports_img_chart128329_0bac125c57dc.jpg
However, July has marked this year's low in monthly iron ore import volume.
